title: Omnisend vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Omnisend and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for your business needs.
h1: Omnisend vs Constant Contact: A Detailed Comparison
intro: Omnisend and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Omnisend specializes in omnichannel marketing for e-commerce brands, while Constant Contact focuses on simplicity for nonprofits and local businesses. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and budget.
choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce store and want integrated email, SMS, and push notifications
You need advanced segmentation and product picker functionality for personalized campaigns
You want to start free and upgrade only when you've grown your subscriber list
choose_b_if: You prefer a simple, straightforward email marketing tool without complex features
You're a nonprofit or local business focused primarily on email marketing
You want event management and social media advertising integrated into your platform

faq.q: Is Omnisend better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Omnisend is better for e-commerce brands seeking omnichannel marketing with SMS and push notifications, while Constant Contact is better for nonprofits and local businesses prioritizing simplicity. The choice depends on your specific business type and feature requirements rather than one being universally superior.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Omnisend or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Omnisend offers a free tier with up to 250 subscribers, making it cheaper for startups and small lists. Constant Contact starts at $12/month with no free option, but Omnisend's paid plans begin at $16/month. For growing businesses, Omnisend's freemium model may be more cost-effective initially.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Omnisend?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms typically support contact list imports through CSV files or direct integrations. Most email marketing platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and migrate to a competitor, though you should review each platform's specific migration documentation for optimal results.
